app.tagline: Swing-trade signals · trend-probability model · walk-forward validated
banner.advisory: Advisory only — the current model does not beat buy & hold. Not financial advice.
philosophy.title: What P(win) means.
philosophy.body: The model doesn't predict a price. P(win) is the probability that, if you enter today, the target (+2 ATR) is hit BEFORE the stop (−1 ATR) within 10 trading days. The strategy rides trends with a 2:1 reward/risk, so the break-even hit rate is ~33%, not 50% — that's why the buy threshold sits at 0.35. Only assets we've already optimized are shown.
banner.experimental: Signals served by a model dedicated to this asset class, but advisory only: not validated — no model beats buy & hold, and with less scrutiny than the US universe.
dashboard.exportDesc: Download the full paper-trade history as CSV for external analysis.
ticker.noNews: No recent news for this ticker.
ticker.noExplain: No explanation available (no signal yet, or model not LightGBM).
ticker.noHistory: No signals recorded yet for this ticker.
hood.title: Under the hood — what BeRich actually does
hood.intro: A complete, current picture of the pipeline: how data is acquired, how features are engineered, how models are trained, selected and tuned, how often everything retrains, and — most importantly — how to read the signals and where stops and targets come from. BeRich is advisory: no model currently beats buy & hold out-of-sample, and that is surfaced honestly throughout.
lang.label: Language
how.title: How BeRich works
how.intro: BeRich estimates a trend probability for each ticker — the chance that the upper barrier (target) is hit before the lower (stop) within N bars. It does not predict a price.
how.section.dataBody: Daily OHLCV bars are ingested from yfinance and cached as Parquet. The scheduler refreshes them every weekday morning.
how.section.featuresBody: 22 hand-rolled causal indicators (returns, SMAs, RSI, ATR, MACD, Bollinger Bands, volume z-scores, cross-asset macro). All built without lookahead.
how.section.labelsBody: Triple-barrier: did the upper barrier (target = entry + k·ATR) hit before the lower (stop = entry − k·ATR) within 10 bars? 1 = win, 0 = loss.
how.section.modelBody: LightGBM baseline today. The model is only promoted if it beats both LightGBM-baseline and equal-weight buy & hold, walk-forward, out-of-sample.
how.section.signalsBody: Daily: BUY if P(win) ≥ 0.55, SELL if ≤ 0.30, NEUTRAL otherwise. Position sizing risks at most 1% of capital to the stop.
how.section.honestyBody: v0.1.0: the baseline does not beat buy & hold on this universe. Surfaced everywhere as 'advisory only'. No edge claim.
light.title: Your ideas today
light.subtitle: A plain-language read of today's signals. Advisory only — not financial advice.
light.stop: Exit if it falls to (safety stop)
light.noIdeas: No clear opportunity today — the model is staying on the sidelines.
light.howReadBody: Green = the model leans positive, grey = unsure, red = leans negative. These are probabilities, not promises. Prices are indicative.
training.title: Per-asset training
training.intro: Each asset is trained and optimized on its own, per side (long / short). A model is only promoted if it beats that asset's own benchmark out-of-sample — otherwise it stays advisory-only. This tab shows what was trained, the winning model, its guard metrics, whether a per-asset hyperparameter search ran, and when.
training.noHpoBanner: No per-asset hyperparameter search has run yet — promoted models use regularized default parameters. The deep weekend sweep (or `berich hpo --ticker ...`) populates per-asset Optuna studies.
ops.title: Machine status
ops.intro: Live view of what the box is doing in the background — GPU load, scheduled jobs, the per-asset HPO queue, and recent scheduler logs. Auto-refreshes every 5 seconds.
